title: Computed styles and layout inspectors deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because DevTools investigation evidence is an observed diagnostic process, not a deterministic final-DOM property.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Computed styles and layout inspectors: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? The Styles panel shows only declarations that currently win, so crossed-out rules can be ignored.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Inspect matched and overridden rules plus computed values; a crossed-out declaration often explains the competing cascade.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Inspect matched and overridden rules plus computed values; a crossed-out declaration often explains the competing cascade.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Inspect matched and overridden rules plus computed values; a crossed-out declaration often explains the competing cascade.
The Styles panel shows only declarations that currently win, so crossed-out rules can be ignored.
